title: HTTP Semantics, Messages, Versions, and the Wire Model · Execution Trace: Semantics Syntax Separation
summary: A raw implementation copies endpoint behavior into its parser, confuses resources with representations, infers message bodies from method names, and treats HTTP/1.1 syntax as universal HTTP semantics. Planned artifact: Add and verify the semantics syntax separation increment in version-neutral message and semantics model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate version-neutral method, status, field, content, and resource semantics from HTTP/1.1 textual serialization.
Treat failed or timed-out writes as potentially partially observed and avoid blind replay of non-idempotent protocol effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Semantics Syntax Separation · Theory Semantics Syntax Separation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 3056 in a incident replay lab retaining minimized request-smuggling corpora. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive message content from method and status semantics before wire framing; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate version-neutral method, status, field, content, and resource semantics from HTTP/1.1 textual serialization.
